patterns.name: reentrancy-eth
patterns.description: External call sends ETH before state update. Attacker re-enters the function before balance is decremented, draining the contract.
indicators: receive() or fallback() in attacker contract
requirements: function sends ETH to external address
state update happens after the call
attacker controls receiving contract
patterns.impact: Complete drain of ETH from contract

patterns.name: reentrancy-token
patterns.description: Reentrancy via ERC777 hooks, ERC1155 callbacks, or custom token transfer hooks. State is read in one function and updated in another, creating a window for manipulation.
indicators: shared storage read across two functions
requirements: token with transfer hook or callback
state not updated before external token transfer
attacker controls token receiver

patterns.name: read-only-reentrancy
patterns.description: During a reentrant call, view functions return stale state. A second protocol reading price or balance from the first protocol during its own state transition gets a manipulated value.
indicators: view function reads balances during ongoing transaction
Curve or Balancer LP price used by lending protocol
no reentrancy lock on view functions
requirements: protocol A calls external contract mid-execution
protocol B reads state from protocol A via view function
attacker controls the reentrant call
patterns.impact: Price manipulation leading to undercollateralized borrows
mitigations: use committed state not transient state

patterns.name: flash-loan-oracle-manipulation
patterns.description: Attacker borrows large amount via flash loan, manipulates a spot price oracle by moving reserves in a pool, exploits a protocol reading that price, then repays.
indicators: price read in same block as swap
requirements: protocol uses spot price from AMM pool
flash loan available for relevant token
price affects borrowing or liquidation
patterns.impact: Undercollateralized borrow, liquidation manipulation, fund drain

patterns.name: price-manipulation-twap
patterns.description: Short TWAP windows can be manipulated over multiple blocks by a well-capitalized attacker. Low liquidity pools are especially vulnerable.
indicators: low liquidity pool as price source
single pool TWAP with no fallback
requirements: TWAP window short enough to manipulate
attacker has capital to sustain price skew
protocol relies on single TWAP source
patterns.impact: Gradual price manipulation enabling undercollateralized borrows
mitigations: circuit breaker on large price movements

patterns.name: access-control-missing
patterns.description: Critical functions lack access control modifiers. Any address can call functions that should be restricted to owner, admin, or governance.
requirements: function is externally callable
function modifies critical state or moves funds
patterns.impact: Unauthorized fund withdrawal, ownership takeover, protocol destruction

patterns.name: controlled-delegatecall
patterns.description: Delegatecall target or calldata is controlled by the caller. Attacker points delegatecall at a malicious contract to execute arbitrary code in the context of the victim contract.
indicators: proxy with no whitelist on implementation
requirements: caller can influence delegatecall target or data
victim contract has valuable state or funds
patterns.impact: Full storage takeover, ownership hijack, fund drain

patterns.name: integer-overflow
patterns.description: Arithmetic operations overflow or underflow without SafeMath or Solidity 0.8+ checks. Balances wrap around to unexpected values.
indicators: balance or supply calculation without bounds
requirements: Solidity < 0.8.0 or unchecked block
arithmetic on user-controlled values
patterns.impact: Balance manipulation, minting unlimited tokens, bypassing limits
mitigations: explicit bounds checking in unchecked blocks

patterns.name: precision-loss
patterns.description: Division before multiplication causes precision loss. In high-value DeFi calculations, rounding errors can be exploited at scale.
requirements: arithmetic involves division
result used in financial calculation
attacker can scale operations
patterns.impact: Rounding exploitation, fee avoidance, share price manipulation
mitigations: minimum deposit amounts to prevent dust attacks

patterns.name: flash-loan-attack
patterns.description: Attacker uses flash loan to temporarily hold large capital, manipulate protocol state, extract value, and repay in a single transaction.
indicators: protocol state readable and changeable in single tx
price or liquidity affects protocol in same block
large single-tx capital requirement feasible via flash loan
requirements: flash loan available for relevant asset
protocol vulnerable to large single-tx capital
profit extractable within single transaction
patterns.impact: Liquidation manipulation, price manipulation, governance takeover

patterns.name: bridge-message-replay
patterns.description: Bridge fails to invalidate processed messages. Attacker replays a valid cross-chain message to trigger multiple executions of a single deposit or mint.
indicators: no nonce or message ID tracking
message hash not stored after execution
requirements: bridge accepts cross-chain messages
no replay protection on message execution
attacker has a valid past message
mitigations: message ID uniqueness check before execution

patterns.name: governance-flash-loan
patterns.description: Attacker borrows governance tokens via flash loan, votes on a malicious proposal in the same transaction, then repays. No time-weighted voting means instant governance takeover.
indicators: voting power from current balance not snapshot
proposal and execution in same block possible
token balance used directly for votes
requirements: governance token available via flash loan
no snapshot-based voting
no timelock or insufficient timelock
patterns.impact: Governance takeover, treasury drain, malicious upgrades
mitigations: snapshot voting power at proposal creation

patterns.name: vault-inflation-attack
patterns.description: Attacker is first depositor in an ERC4626 vault. Donates assets directly to vault before victim deposits. Share price inflates, victim receives 0 shares due to rounding.
indicators: no virtual shares or dead shares protection
first depositor can set initial share price
requirements: vault uses ERC4626 share model
attacker can be first depositor
no minimum deposit or virtual share protection
patterns.impact: Victim loses entire deposit to attacker

patterns.name: liquidation-manipulation
patterns.description: Attacker manipulates collateral price or borrow amount to trigger or avoid liquidation. Can also grief other users by making their positions unliquidatable.
indicators: liquidation bonus large enough to profit from manipulation
requirements: price oracle manipulable
attacker has open position or can open one
liquidation bonus exists
patterns.impact: Unfair liquidation of victim positions, protocol insolvency
mitigations: circuit breaker on large price moves

patterns.name: unchecked-return-value
patterns.description: ERC20 transfer or transferFrom return value not checked. Non-standard tokens that return false on failure instead of reverting cause silent accounting errors.
requirements: token used does not revert on failure
protocol does not check transfer return value
patterns.impact: Silent transfer failure, accounting mismatch, fund loss
